content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

The score of a permutation PPP of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N] is defined as follows:


If Pi=iP_i = iPi​=i for at least one iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the score is −1-1−1.
Otherwise, the score is the number of indices iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N) such that APi≤AiA_{P_i} \le A_iAPi​​≤Ai​.
Find the maximum possible score for some permutation PPP. You also need to print a valid permutation.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output the following:



On a new line, first output the maximum possible score.
Next, output NNN integers, P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​, any valid permutation that obtains the maximum score.

If multiple answers are possible, all will be accepted.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Any permutation PPP which does not have Pi=iP_i = iPi​=i for any index iii, would have a score of 333 because all values are 111, so APi≤AiA_{P_i} \le A_iAPi​​≤Ai​ always holds.
Test Case 2 : P=[3,1,2]P = [3, 1, 2]P=[3,1,2] means that index i=1,3i = 1, 3i=1,3 are good because AP1=A3=2≤A1A_{P_1} = A_3 = 2 \le A_1AP1​​=A3​=2≤A1​, and AP3=A2=1≤A3A_{P_3} = A_2 = 1 \le A_3AP3​​=A2​=1≤A3​. It can be shown that a larger score is impossible.
